'''Jander Graffe''' was the [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] of [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sector]].

==Biography==
Jander Graffe was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] who served the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].<ref name="ITCW" /> Prior to [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]],<ref name="GNN3" /> Graffe was assigned to control a [[Sector/Legends|sector]] in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]] where he established a reputation for imposing the [[Emperor/Legends|Emperor's]] will with an iron fist and direct applications of fear and aggression. Around 0 BBY, Graffe was personally assigned to control the [[Darpa sector/Legends|Darpa sector]] in the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core]], which was one of the sectors comprising the [[Ringali Shell]], by Emperor [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]. The Emperor was concerned that several planets in the sector—[[Ralltiir/Legends|Ralltiir]] in particular—were showing signs of [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] sympathies, and knew that Graffe was capable of handling any problems that arose.<ref name="ITCW" /> Graffe immediately ordered an Imperial force to [[Subjugation of Ralltiir|invade]] and [[Blockade of Ralltiir|blockade]] Ralltiir to cleanse the world of Rebels. The leader of the occupation, [[Lord/Legends|Lord]] [[Tion (Lord)/Legends|Tion]], turned the planet over to [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Dennix Graeber]], a protégé of Graffe's who has been transferred to Ralltiir at the Moff's request.<ref name="FOP">''[[The Far Orbit Project]]''</ref> Graffe and Graeber shared a desire to oppress their populations and enforce harsh laws. Unlike Graffe, who was bound by the needs to keep his sector running smoothly, Graeber was able to apply force to the [[Ralltiiri]] population.<ref name="ITCW" />

Based on [[Esseles/Legends|Esseles]], Moff Graffe lived in the official Moff's residence,<ref name="FOP" /> [[Esprix Estate]].<ref name="CCW">''[[Coruscant and the Core Worlds]]''</ref> Graffe's new position in a Core World sector forced the Moff to moderate his approach. He clashed with several local governors about his methods, but they dared not confront him directly.<ref name="ITCW" /> In [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], Graffe was busy replacing the bureaucracy on Esseles established by [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Gabrial Atanna]] due to the [[Dissolution of the Imperial Senate/Legends|dissolution]] of the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]].<ref name="GNN3" /> Members of the [[Esselian New Order Party]] worked closely with the offices of Moff Graffe and Governor [[Griff Takel]] to coordinate pro-Imperial events on Esseles. During his tenure, Graffe attempted to persuade [[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Phadreas Kole]] of [[Rhinnal/Legends|Rhinnal]] to bring his world more into the Empire, but did not make much headway.<ref name="ITCW" /> Since the Governor's inactivity did nothing to threaten the Empire, he was left alone.<ref name="FOP" />

In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], Rebel forces on Esseles shut down the [[Calamar Power Plant]] in [[Calamar]], which threw the city into chaos. Moff Graffe urged Governor Takel to investigate the matter immediately.<ref name="GNN8">{{JournalCite|8|Galaxywide NewsNets}}</ref>

During the Galactic Civil War, Emperor Palpatine issued secret orders to his Core World Moffs. To raise taxes in their sectors, Moffs were ordered to instigate alien-led "riots", which could then be used as the justification for a new security tax. Graffe convened a meeting of government officials and pro-Imperial supporters to plan the acts on Esseles.<ref name="CCW" />

{{RPGseed}}
Local [[NewsNet]] [[Journalist/Legends|reporter]] and Alliance [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Deena Mipps]] learned of the meeting, and led a group of Rebel agents to secretly record the conference. After bypassing security and the Moff's two [[Chiba DR-10 protocol droid]]s, they recorded the discussion about harming alien residents on Esseles, and later broadcast the [[Holovid/Legends|holovid]] to embarrass the Moff.<ref name="CCW" />
{{Endgame}}

With support for the Rebellion growing, Graffe intended to commit a series of atrocities, and blame them on the Alliance. He contacted [[Garthus Hal]], an Outer Rim criminal that Graffe had met earlier in his career on a backwater posting. Hal's targets included bombing a [[Monorail/Legends|monorail]] station and blaming it on an alien power, destroying a concert hall in the name of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]], and killing several popular [[Podracer/Legends|Podracers]] at the [[Togatto Speedway/Legends|Togatto Speedway]]. The citizenry called for protection, and the Alliance was forced to send a team after the bombers to stop them from doing any more damage.<ref name="CCW" />

{{RPGseed}}
Graffe protected the bombers, making it hard for the Alliance team to find them. However, they switched their attention from the bombers to the Moff himself, following Graffe to a meeting with Hal in [[New Calamar]]. The Alliance team then neutralized the threat once the bombers had been located and identified.<ref name="CCW" />
{{Endgame}}

==Personality and traits==
Jander Graffe was totally loyal to the Empire, and was eager to employ its edicts and decrees against the populations he was assigned to rule.<ref name="FOP" /> Although allowed a freer hand in the Outer Rim,<ref name="ITCW" /> he had to rein himself in to keep the Darpa sector under control by using more political methods. Graffe was considered to be a dark and twisted individual.<ref name="FOP" />

==Behind the scenes==
In ''[[Galaxywide NewsNets]]'' in [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 3|''Star Wars Adventure Journal'' 3]], his name is spelled "Jander Graff."
